summary: The thalamus relays all sensory information to the cerebral cortex except olfaction (smell).
The thalamus relays all sensory information including smell to the cortex.
The hypothalamus is the relay station for all sensory signals.
The hypothalamus is the master regulator of homeostasis, controlling temperature, hunger, thirst, and the autonomic nervous system.
The thalamus is the master regulator of homeostasis.
The hypothalamus only controls body temperature and has no role in the autonomic nervous system.
Upper motor neuron lesions cause spastic paralysis with hyperreflexia, while lower motor neuron lesions cause flaccid paralysis.
Upper motor neuron lesions cause flaccid paralysis with hyporeflexia.
Both upper and lower motor neuron lesions cause spastic paralysis.
In Parkinson's disease, dopamine is depleted in the substantia nigra of the basal ganglia.
In Parkinson's disease, serotonin is depleted in the raphe nuclei.
In Parkinson's disease, acetylcholine is depleted in the cerebellum.
The reticular activating system in the brainstem maintains consciousness and wakefulness.
The limbic system maintains consciousness and wakefulness.
The cerebellum is responsible for maintaining consciousness.
Wernicke's area in the superior temporal gyrus is responsible for language comprehension.
Wernicke's area in the frontal lobe is responsible for language comprehension.
Broca's area in the temporal lobe is responsible for language comprehension.
Brown-Sequard syndrome results from hemisection of the spinal cord, causing ipsilateral motor loss and contralateral pain/temperature loss.
Brown-Sequard syndrome results from complete transection of the spinal cord.
Brown-Sequard syndrome causes bilateral motor and sensory loss below the lesion.
The Gate Control Theory states that large-diameter A-beta fibres can inhibit pain transmission by small C fibres at the spinal level.
The Gate Control Theory states that C fibres inhibit A-beta fibre transmission.
The Gate Control Theory describes how the brain generates pain signals independently of peripheral input.
